quote: Though we cannot think alike, may we not love alike?
source: Sermon 39: Catholic Spirit

quote: If your heart is as my heart, give me your hand.
source: Sermon 39: Catholic Spirit

quote: I look upon all the world as my parish.
source: Journal, June 11, 1739

quote: Earn all you can, save all you can, give all you can.
source: Sermon 50: The Use of Money

quote: The gospel of Christ knows of no religion but social; no holiness but social holiness.
source: Preface to Hymns and Sacred Poems (1739)

quote: Best of all, God is with us.
source: Last Words, March 2, 1791

quote: By salvation I mean, not barely deliverance from hell, but a present deliverance from sin.
source: Sermon 43: The Scripture Way of Salvation

quote: The Bible knows nothing of solitary religion.
source: Preface to Hymns and Sacred Poems (1739)

quote: Do all the good you can, by all the means you can, in all the ways you can, in all the places you can, at all the times you can, to all the people you can, as long as ever you can.
source: Attributed Methodist Rule of Life

quote: When I have money, I get rid of it quickly, lest it find a way into my heart.
source: Journal

quote: I am a creature of a day, passing through life as an arrow through the air.
source: Preface to Sermons on Several Occasions

quote: I want to know one thing, the way to heaven; how to land safe on that happy shore.
source: Preface to Sermons on Several Occasions

quote: Orthodoxy, or right opinion, is, at best, but a very slender part of religion.
source: Sermon 17: The Circumcision of the Heart

quote: God does nothing but by prayer, and everything with it.
source: Letter to Rev. John Trembath (1748)

quote: To serve God and do good to all is the sum of religion.
source: Paraphrase from Wesley Journals

quote: Leisure and I have taken leave of one another.
source: Journal

quote: Give me one hundred preachers who fear nothing but sin and desire nothing but God, and I care not a straw whether they be clergymen or laymen; such alone will shake the gates of hell and set up the kingdom of heaven on earth.
source: Letter to Alexander Mather (1777)

quote: The world is my parish.
source: Journal, June 1739

quote: God buries His workmen, but carries on His work.
source: Common Wesleyan Saying

quote: Is thine heart right, as my heart is with thy heart? If it be, give me thine hand.
source: Sermon 39: Catholic Spirit

quote: All the blessings which God hath bestowed upon man are of his mere grace, bounty, or favour; his free, undeserved favour; favour altogether undeserved; man having no claim to the least of his mercies.
source: Sermon 1: Salvation by Faith

quote: And many there are who go thus far: ever since the Christian religion was in the world, there have been many in every age and nation who were almost persuaded to be Christians.
source: Sermon 2: The Almost Christian

quote: Awake, thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall give thee light.
source: Sermon 3: Awake, Thou That Sleepest

quote: When the day of Pentecost was fully come, they were all with one accord in one place.
source: Sermon 4: Scriptural Christianity

quote: How a sinner may be justified before God, the Lord and Judge of all, is a question of no common importance to every child of man.
source: Sermon 5: Justification by Faith

quote: But the righteousness which is of faith speaketh on this wise, Say not in thine heart, Who shall ascend into heaven?
source: Sermon 6: The Righteousness of Faith

quote: Not in any outward thing; such as forms, or ceremonies, even of the most excellent kind.
source: Sermon 7: The Way to the Kingdom

quote: There is no condemnation to them from God; for he hath justified them “freely by his grace, through the redemption that is in Jesus.” he hath forgiven all their iniquities, and blotted out all their sins.
source: Sermon 8: The First-Fruits of the Spirit

quote: Perhaps most of you, who, by the mercy of God, now partake of a better spirit, may remember the time when ye were as they, when ye were under the same condemnation.
source: Sermon 9: The Spirit of Bondage and of Adoption

quote: How many vain men, not understanding what they spake, neither whereof they affirmed, have wrested this Scripture to the great loss if not the destruction of their souls!
source: Sermon 10: The Witness of the Spirit, Discourse I

quote: And it is the more necessary to explain and defend this truth, because there is a danger on the right hand and on the left.
source: Sermon 11: The Witness of the Spirit, Discourse II

quote: Such is the voice of every true believer in Christ, so long as he abides in faith and love.
source: Sermon 12: The Witness of Our Own Spirit

quote: Is there any sin in them that are born of God, or are they wholly delivered from it?
source: Sermon 13: On Sin in Believers

quote: It is generally supposed, that repentance and faith are only the gate of religion; that they are necessary only at the beginning of our Christian course, when we are setting out in the way to the kingdom.
source: Sermon 14: The Repentance of Believers

quote: The occasion likewise of this assembly adds not a little to the solemnity of it: to hear and determine causes of every kind, some of which are of the most important nature; on which depends no less than life or death, death that uncovers the face of eternity!
source: Sermon 15: The Great Assize

quote: But are there any ordinances now, since life and immortality were brought to light by the gospel?
source: Sermon 16: The Means of Grace

quote: A hard saying this to the natural man, Who is alive unto the world, and dead unto God; and one that he will not readily be persuaded to receive as the truth of God, unless it be so qualified in the interpretation, as to have neither use nor significance left.
source: Sermon 17: The Circumcision of the Heart

quote: How is every one that is “born of the Spirit,” — that is, born again, — born of God?
source: Sermon 18: The Marks of the New Birth

quote: But though it be allowed, that justification and the new birth are, in point of time, inseparable from each other, yet are they easily distinguished, as being not the same, but things of a widely different nature.
source: Sermon 19: The Great Privilege of those that are Born of God

quote: How dreadful and how innumerable are the contests which have arisen about religion!
source: Sermon 20: The Lord Our Righteousness

quote: And seeing the multitudes, he went up into a mountain, where there was room for all that came unto him, from every quarter.
source: Sermon 21: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, I

quote: Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: For they shall be filled.
source: Sermon 22: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, II

quote: Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness' sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
source: Sermon 23: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, III

quote: It is thenceforth good for nothing but to be cast out, and trodden under foot of men.
source: Sermon 24: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, IV

quote: For verily I say unto you: Till heaven and earth pass, one jot or one tittle shall in no wise pass from the law, till all be fulfilled.
source: Sermon 25: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, V

quote: Therefore when thou doest thine alms, do not sound a trumpet before thee, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may have glory of men.
source: Sermon 26: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, VI

quote: When thou fastest, anoint thine head, and wash thy face; that thou appear not unto men to fast, but unto thy Father which is in secret.
source: Sermon 27: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, VII

quote: The light of the body is the eye: if therefore thine eye be single, thy whole body shall be full of light.
source: Sermon 28: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, VIII

quote: Therefore I say unto you, Take no thought for your life, what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink; nor yet for your body, what ye shall put on.
source: Sermon 29: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, IX

quote: For with what judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged; and with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you again.
source: Sermon 30: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, X

quote: By one or the other of these, thousands, who once ran well, have drawn back unto perdition; — yea, many of those who were not novices in religion, who had made some progress in righteousness.
source: Sermon 31: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, XI

quote: Even so every good tree bringeth forth good fruit, but a corrupt tree bringeth forth evil fruit.
source: Sermon 32: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, XII

quote: Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in thy name?
source: Sermon 33: Upon Our Lord's Sermon on the Mount, XIII

quote: Perhaps there are few subjects within the whole compass of religion so little understood as this.
source: Sermon 34: The Original, Nature, Property and Use of the Law

quote: He speaks particularly of salvation from the guilt of sin, which he commonly terms justification.
source: Sermon 35: The Law Established through Faith, Discourse I

quote: We do not, indeed, establish the old ceremonial law; we know that is abolished for ever.
source: Sermon 36: The Law Established through Faith, Discourse II

quote: And so say all the world, the men who know not God, of all that are of Paul’s religion: of every one who is so a follower of him as he was of Christ.
source: Sermon 37: The Nature of Enthusiasm

quote: Has not the power of doing this been withdrawn from the church, for twelve or fourteen hundred years?
source: Sermon 38: A Caution Against Bigotry

quote: A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another: as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.
source: Sermon 39: Catholic Spirit

quote: There is scarce any expression in Holy Writ which has given more offence than this.
source: Sermon 40: Christian Perfection

quote: But will God so “bring every thought into captivity to the obedience of Christ,” that no wandering thought will find a place in the mind, even while we remain in the body?
source: Sermon 41: Wandering Thoughts

quote: But it is of one of them only that I now propose to speak, (although exerted in various ways,) whereby he endeavours to divide the gospel against itself, and by one part of it to overthrow the other.
source: Sermon 42: Satan's Devices

quote: Nothing can be more intricate, complex, and hard to be understood, than religion, as it has been often described.
source: Sermon 43: The Scripture Way of Salvation

quote: How widely different is this from the fair pictures of human nature which men have drawn in all ages!
source: Sermon 44: Original Sin
